prompt:
I maintain the queue software for a shared 0.7m observatory and the run lifecycle is currently a mutable class with an enum field and a pile of if/else that nobody trusts. I'm rewriting it and I have strong opinions about the shape: states and events are immutable records, and the whole lifecycle is ONE pure transition function built on switch expressions over the (state, event) pair — C# pattern matching is the entire point of this rewrite. No mutable state class, no transition dictionary, no timestamps anywhere (the scheduler owns wall time; elapsed time arrives as integer minutes inside events). .NET 10, namespace ObsRun, single project.

Types to define, exactly these shapes (tests construct them positionally and compare by value):
- abstract record ObsState(string TargetId), with sealed derived records: Drafted(string TargetId, int RequestedMinutes); Queued(string TargetId, int RequestedMinutes, int Priority); Observing(string TargetId, int RequestedMinutes, int ElapsedMinutes); Suspended(string TargetId, int RequestedMinutes, int ElapsedMinutes, string Reason); Completed(string TargetId, int TotalMinutes); Scrubbed(string TargetId, string Reason).
- abstract record ObsEvent, with sealed derived records: Approve(int Priority); StartExposure; Progress(int Minutes); WeatherHold(string Reason); Resume; Scrub(string Reason).
- sealed class InvalidTransitionException : InvalidOperationException, carrying the offending pair as properties ObsState State and ObsEvent Event (the same instances that were passed in), Message exactly "cannot apply {event type name} in {state type name}", e.g. "cannot apply StartExposure in Completed".
- static class RunController with: ObsState Apply(ObsState state, ObsEvent evt); ObsState Replay(ObsState initial, IEnumerable<ObsEvent> events) (fold Apply over the sequence; empty sequence returns the initial state); bool IsTerminal(ObsState state); string Describe(ObsState state).

The lifecycle: Drafted --Approve--> Queued (priority copied in, RequestedMinutes carried over). Queued --StartExposure--> Observing with ElapsedMinutes 0. Observing --Progress(m)--> accumulate: if ElapsedMinutes + m is still under RequestedMinutes stay Observing with the new total (non-destructive — the old state value must be unchanged); once it reaches or passes RequestedMinutes the run is Completed with TotalMinutes capped at exactly RequestedMinutes (we never bill past the request). Observing --WeatherHold(reason)--> Suspended, keeping the elapsed minutes; Suspended --Resume--> back to Observing with the same elapsed minutes. Scrub(reason) is allowed from Drafted, Queued, Observing, and Suspended and lands in Scrubbed. Completed and Scrubbed are terminal: EVERY event there throws InvalidTransitionException. Any other (state, event) combination throws InvalidTransitionException too — the tests sweep the full 6x6 matrix, so handle the whole space deliberately.

Argument sanity, checked ONLY when the pair itself is legal (an illegal pair always reports the transition problem first): Approve.Priority must be 1..5 inclusive, Progress.Minutes must be positive; violations throw ArgumentOutOfRangeException.

Describe returns the status-board line per state: "NGC-7000: drafted (90m requested)" / "NGC-7000: queued p2 (90m)" / "NGC-7000: observing 40/90m" / "NGC-7000: on hold at 40/90m (cirrus band)" / "NGC-7000: complete (90m)" / "NGC-7000: scrubbed (dome ice)" — same shape for any target/numbers/reason. Make it a switch expression too.

ObsRunTests.cs is the acceptance contract; leave it, the csproj, and .gitignore alone. `dotnet test` green = done.
